description: Single authoritative Challenge/Career landing score. Phase scores are pure metric quality; every penalty multiplies the combined ranked score.
formula: subtotal = sum(phaseScore * phaseWeightPercent / 100); final = round(subtotal * product(general penalty multipliers), 1)

contactStability.penaltyDescription: The initial landing is the baseline. One valid airborne/recontact cycle (the second touchdown) multiplies the combined score by 0.9; two or more cycles (the third touchdown or later) multiply it by 0.8.

gear.penaltyDescription: If gear is required and up, multiply the combined score by 0.1. Gear down is the required baseline and awards no points.

flaps.penaltyDescription: If flaps are not set for landing (index below min / above max), multiply the combined score by 0.8. Correct flaps award no points.

spoilerDeployment.penaltyDescription: Both left and right spoiler surfaces must deploy at least 15% no later than two seconds after the accepted main-gear touchdown; failure multiplies the combined score.

noseGearImpact.penaltyDescription: A moderate nose-gear impact multiplies the combined score by 0.95; a severe impact multiplies it by 0.9. The worst verified nose contact or recontact applies once.

stallWarning.penaltyDescription: If the aircraft-native STALL WARNING state activates at any time after the attempt is armed, multiply the combined score by 0.9. No warning is the required baseline and awards no points.

overspeedWarning.penaltyDescription: If the aircraft-native OVERSPEED WARNING state activates at any time after the attempt is armed, multiply the combined score by 0.9. No warning is the required baseline and awards no points.

automation.penaltyDescription: Heading and altitude hold must be off at or below 2000 ft RA. AP master/AP1/AP2 and active or armed autothrust must be off at or below 1000 ft RA; failure multiplies the combined score. Flight directors may remain on.

manualBraking.penaltyDescription: Manual brake pedals must remain released while the nose gear is airborne. Within three seconds of nose-gear touchdown either both pedals must be applied or autobrake must be active. Failure multiplies the combined score.

rollout.penaltyDescription: As soon as groundspeed falls below the settle threshold, remaining runway must be at least max(400 m, 15% of runway length). Falling short multiplies the combined score by 0.85.

reverseThrust.penaltyDescription: Every engine operating at main-gear touchdown must select at least idle reverse no later than TD+5 seconds. Powered reverse must be reduced to reverse idle or stowed by 60 kt, and reverse idle may remain selected until all reversers must be completely stowed by 30 kt. Failure multiplies the combined score once.

pauseUsage.penaltyDescription: Normal pause and Active Pause are prohibited from the first unpaused challenge-flight sample through main-gear touchdown. This general factor applies to the combined score.

simulationRate.penaltyDescription: Reducing the simulation rate below normal speed before touchdown applies this general factor to the combined score once.

cockpitView.penaltyDescription: Each transition from cockpit view (CAMERA STATE 2) to any exterior or other non-cockpit camera multiplies the combined score by 0.97. Multiple exits stack multiplicatively from the first unpaused challenge sample through main-gear touchdown.

metrics.note: Initial main-gear impact only. Combines the contact-edge VERTICAL SPEED bracket mean (pilot-facing sink rate) and robust filtered peak-G using weighted RMS penalties. MSFS ground-normal velocity is retained only as a diagnostic.
Main-gear touchdown relative to the runway aiming-point marker. The ideal band is 300-500 ft beyond the marker, with an 800 ft short-side falloff and a steeper 500 ft long-side falloff.
Measures actual runway consumed after a sustained near-level or positive vertical-speed state begins below flare height. IAS scoring is not reused.
Target-accuracy metric. The target is VAPP minus 5 kt by default. Being fast is already punished more heavily than being slow.
Touchdown lateral offset. Full score within 2 m of centerline; zero near runway edge on a typical narrow runway.

Heading and ground track are scored independently against the runway, then combined 50/50 by penalty RMS. True crab (heading minus track) is shown separately as a diagnostic. Touchdown carries 70%; time-integrated TD+3-second control carries 30%.

phases.note: Short final (≈0.2–4.5 NM, includes typical 4 NM spawn): runway-specific path match, vertical steadiness, lateral steadiness, and bank stability. Bank is 20%; the three path metrics share the remaining 80%.

metrics.note: Time-weighted angular deviation vs the runway glideslope path (default 3°; challenge-set; free flight prefers curated steep-approach catalog, then VASI/PAPI). The normalized unflared aim point is 1,000 ft past the landing threshold. Below-path deviation counts ×0.35; above-path deviation counts ×2.0, so low approaches receive generous tolerance while high/deep approaches are penalized strongly. Opposite deviations cannot cancel.
Total vertical path pumping: Σ|Δe| / T. Steady path scores high; large corrections and oscillations score low.

Total lateral path change: Σ|Δd| / ground distance. Straight track scores high; big corrections and S-turns score low. Full credit now requires exceptionally steady tracking (≤0.004 m/m), with zero by 0.08 m/m.
Time-weighted mean absolute bank on short final: ∫|φ|dt / T. Wings level scores high (full credit within 0.5°); sustained bank and left/right rocking score low (zero by 8° mean |bank|).

phases.note: Centerline path, weave, and peak lateral deviation share the phase. Combined runway alignment is scored in Touchdown.

metrics.note: Distance-weighted mean |offset| on rollout. Full score within 2 m of centerline; zero near a typical runway edge (~half-width).
